A supplement to the pharmacopoeia : being a treatise on pharmacology in general; including not only the drugs and compounds which are used by practitioners of medicine, but also those which are sold by chemists, druggists, and herbalists, for other purposes; together with a collection of the most useful medical formulæ ; an explanation of the contractions used by physicians and druggists; the medical arrangement of the articles of the London pharmacopeia, with their doses, at one view; a similar list of the indigenous plants of the British islands, which are capable of being used in medicine, &c. ; and also a very copious index, English and Latin, of the various names by which the articles have been known at different periods. / by Samuel Frederick Gray.

retrieval_text: Mallow is a medicinal herb profile in the Herbalisti historical corpus. It is represented across 283 works and 542 source-linked chunks. Recorded variants include Mallow, Mallows, Mallowes. Common plant-part co-mentions include Flower, Root, Seed, Leaf, Gum, Berry. Common preparation co-mentions include Decoction, Oil, Tea, Poultice, Syrup, Infusion. Common condition co-mentions include Inflammation, Cold or catarrh, Wounds or ulcers, Fever, Cough, Vomiting or nausea. Caution co-mentions include Bleeding caution, Dose caution, Fatal-risk warning, Narcotic or sedative caution, Child-use caution, Allergy or sensitivity caution. Representative source works include The pharmacopoeia universalis or, complete encyclopedia of the materia medica; contained in the pharmacopoeias of London, Edinburgh, and Dublin as well as of all those of Europe and America ; and of the dispensatories, formularies and chemical works ... With an appendix ... / by A. J. L. Jourdan : Edited by J. Rennie.; Domestic medicine , or, A treatise on the prevention and cure of diseases by regimen and simple medicines : with an appendix, containing a dispensatory for the use of private practitioners; Dr. Mansperger's book of hygiene : containing instructions for the cure of diseases, together with many valuable and useful prescriptions : added to which is an accurate description of the properties and application of the curative powers of roots and herbs, with instructions for a diagnosis of diseases from the urine and pulse; Complete domestic medicine : A treatise on the prevention and cure of diseases, by regimen and simple medicines, with the latest corrections and improvements / [William Buchan].. This is a retrieval summary of historical co-mentions only, not a medical claim.
